display_text: Phoenix is a Ranged Intelligence hero with low base stats: 23 Strength, 12 Agility, and 16 Intelligence. It has a very slow base attack time of 1.7 seconds and a turn rate of 0.25. This makes last-hitting and trading in lane mechanically difficult, and the low Agility gives poor base armor.
search_text: Phoenix stats ranged intelligence hero 23 str 12 agi 16 int 1.7 attack time 0.25 turn rate low armor attack speed

display_text: Phoenix has two Facets: Immolate and Hotspot. Immolate is a new ability that replaces Sun Ray's health cost, granting it a cooldown and making Phoenix a more aggressive nuker. Hotspot modifies Sun Ray, causing it to deal more damage and apply more Blinding Sun stacks towards the tip of the beam, rewarding precise long-range positioning.
search_text: Phoenix facets Immolate Hotspot Facet Immolate replaces Sun Ray health cost cooldown aggressive nuker Hotspot Sun Ray more damage Blinding Sun stacks tip beam long range positioning

display_text: In lane, use Icarus Dive primarily for positioning and escape, not for damage, as its HP cost is significant. Focus on using Fire Spirits from level 2 or 3 to harass the enemy carry, applying a 20/40/60/80 DPS burn and a massive 50/80/110/140 attack speed slow for 4 seconds, crippling their last-hit ability. Your low base damage makes securing creeps hard; prioritize regen and levels.
search_text: Phoenix laning phase early game Icarus Dive escape positioning Fire Spirits harass 20 40 60 80 dps burn 50 80 110 140 attack speed slow 4 seconds last hits low damage

display_text: Key power spikes: Level 6 for Supernova, which is a massive teamfight and zoning tool requiring 6/8/10 hero hits to destroy and stunning for 1.6/2.0/2.4 seconds. Level 12 with Aghanim's Scepter allows global Sun Ray saves. Level 20 with +2 Supernova Hit Count or +1000 Icarus Dive Cast Range dramatically increases survivability or initiation range. Level 25 with +1.5% Max Health Sun Ray Damage makes Sun Ray lethal against high-HP heroes.
search_text: Phoenix power spikes level 6 Supernova 6 8 10 hero hits 1.6 2.0 2.4 second stun level 12 Aghanim Scepter global Sun Ray level 20 talent +2 Supernova hits +1000 Icarus Dive cast range level 25 talent +1.5% max health Sun Ray damage

display_text: Phoenix is hard countered by heroes with instant disables or high attack speed. Instant hex or stun (Lion, Shadow Shaman) prevents Supernova cast. High attack speed heroes (Ursa, Troll Warlord, Windranger) can destroy the Supernova egg in seconds. Break (Silver Edge, Hoodwink) disables the innate Blinding Sun passive. Pure damage nukes (Timbersaw) bypass Phoenix's high HP pool from Strength items.
search_text: Phoenix counters hard counter instant hex stun Lion Shadow Shaman Supernova high attack speed Ursa Troll Windranger destroy egg Break Silver Edge Hoodwink Blinding Sun passive Pure damage Timbersaw

display_text: Core items are Tranquil Boots for lane sustain and mobility, and Urn of Shadows/Spirit Vessel. Urn charges are easy to acquire with Fire Spirits and Icarus Dive assists, and the heal synergizes with Phoenix's HP-cost spells. Aghanim's Scepter is essential later, enabling global Sun Ray saves and repositioning.
search_text: Phoenix core build Tranquil Boots Urn Shadows Spirit Vessel Aghanim Scepter heal HP cost spells global Sun Ray save

display_text: In teamfights, Phoenix's role is disruption and area denial. Initiate with Icarus Dive to slow enemies, then launch Fire Spirits on key physical attackers to cripple their DPS. Use Sun Ray to heal allies and damage enemies from a safe distance. The ultimate goal is to force enemies to focus the Supernova egg, zoning them out or setting up for a massive 1.6/2.0/2.4 second AOE stun.
search_text: Phoenix teamfight role 5v5 disruption area denial Icarus Dive slow Fire Spirits attack speed slow Sun Ray heal damage Supernova egg zoning AOE stun 1.6 2.0 2.4 seconds

display_text: Icarus Dive (Q). Phoenix dives forward 1100/1200/1300/1400 units in an arc, dealing 20/40/60/80 damage per second and slowing movement speed by 10%/15%/20%/25% to enemies in a 500-wide path for 4 seconds. The dive lasts 2.0 seconds and can be recast to stop early. Costs 15% of current HP.
search_text: Phoenix ability Q Icarus Dive dash 1100 1200 1300 1400 range 80 dps burn 25% slow 4 seconds 2 sec duration stop cancel 15% HP cost

display_text: Fire Spirits (W). Summons 4 spirits orbiting Phoenix. Each can be launched to a target area, dealing 20/40/60/80 damage per second and reducing attack speed by 50/80/110/140 for 4 seconds in a 200 radius. Costs 20% of current HP. Spirits last 20 seconds. Reveals area for 1 second on impact.
search_text: Phoenix ability W Fire Spirits 4 spirits launch 20 40 60 80 dps 50 80 110 140 attack speed slow 4 seconds 200 radius 20% HP cost reveal

display_text: Sun Ray (E). Channels a beam for up to 6 seconds, costing 5% HP per second. Deals 15/20/25/30 base damage plus 1.5%/3.0%/4.5%/6.0% of the target's max health per second to enemies. Heals allies for 7/10/13/16 base heal plus 0.5%/1.0%/1.5%/2.0% of their max health per second. Has 130 width, 900 range. Phoenix can move at 250 speed and turn at 25 rate while channeling. Cancelled by stun/silence/hex/cyclone/sleep.
search_text: Phoenix ability E Sun Ray channel beam 15 30 base damage 1.5% 6% max health damage heal 7 16 base 0.5% 2% max health heal 130 width 900 range 5% HP cost per second move 250 speed turn 25 rate cancel stun silence hex

display_text: Toggle Movement (F). Toggles slow forward movement (250 speed) during Sun Ray.
search_text: Phoenix ability F Toggle Movement Sun Ray move forward 250 speed

display_text: Blinding Sun (Innate). Debuffs from Phoenix's abilities apply a stackable 2% miss chance per second, lasting 4 seconds.
search_text: Phoenix ability innate passive Blinding Sun 2% miss chance per second stackable 4 second duration

display_text: Supernova (R). Transforms Phoenix into an invulnerable sun for 6 seconds, dealing 60/90/120 damage per second in a 1200 radius. The sun can be destroyed by 6/8/10 attacks from enemy Heroes. Upon expiration or destruction, it explodes, stunning all nearby enemies for 1.6/2.0/2.4 seconds and fully restoring Phoenix's health and mana (refreshes abilities). Provides a Strong Dispel on cast.
search_text: Phoenix ability R ultimate Supernova egg 6 seconds invulnerable 60 90 120 dps 1200 radius 6 8 10 hero hits to destroy 1.6 2.4 second stun full heal mana refresh strong dispel

display_text: When playing from behind, focus on defensive Sun Ray healing and saving allies. Use Supernova as a zoning tool to delay high ground pushes rather than as an offensive initiation. Itemize for utility and saves like Force Staff, Glimmer Cape, or Aghanim's Scepter for global presence.
search_text: Phoenix scenario playing from behind defensive Sun Ray heal save allies Supernova zoning delay high ground push utility items Force Staff Glimmer Cape Aghanim Scepter global

display_text: Against heroes with high attack speed (Ursa, Troll), prioritize Shard upgrade for Sun Ray slow and max Fire Spirits early. The attack speed slow from Fire Spirits is crucial to survive engagements and protect your Supernova egg.
search_text: Phoenix scenario vs high attack speed Ursa Troll Warlord Shard Sun Ray slow max Fire Spirits first 140 attack speed slow protect Supernova egg

display_text: When your team lacks initiation, use Icarus Dive to start fights by diving over the frontline and applying Fire Spirits to the backline. Follow up with Supernova in the middle of the enemy team to force disarray. Build items like Shiva's Guard for the slow aura and armor.
search_text: Phoenix scenario team lacks initiation Icarus Dive start fight Fire Spirits backline Supernova middle enemy team force disarray Shiva Guard slow aura armor

display_text: At Level 10, choose between +25% Icarus Dive Slow (increasing slow from 25% to 31.25%) or +0.5% Blinding Sun Miss Chance (increasing miss chance per second from 2% to 2.5%). Pick the slow if you need more control in fights or for chasing. Pick the miss chance if you are against heavy right-click cores and need to reduce their physical DPS.
search_text: Phoenix talent level 10 left +25% Icarus Dive Slow 31.25% slow right +0.5% Blinding Sun Miss Chance 2.5% per second control chase vs right click physical DPS

display_text: At Level 15, choose between +20 Fire Spirits Damage Per Second (increasing DPS from 80 to 100) or +25 Health Regen. Pick the damage if you are ahead and want to increase your kill threat and wave clear. Pick the massive health regen if you are being focused or need to sustain through constant HP costs from your spells.
search_text: Phoenix talent level 15 left +20 Fire Spirits DPS 100 damage per second right +25 Health Regen kill threat wave clear vs sustain HP cost survival

display_text: At Level 20, choose between +0.6s Supernova Stun Duration (increasing stun from 2.4s to 3.0s) or +2 Supernova Hit Count (increasing required hits from 10 to 12). Pick the longer stun for incredible teamfight lockdown, especially if you have follow-up AOE. Pick the extra hit count against teams with high attack speed heroes, making your egg significantly harder to destroy.
search_text: Phoenix talent level 20 left +0.6s Supernova Stun 3.0 second stun lockdown right +2 Supernova Hit Count 12 hero hits destroy egg vs high attack speed

display_text: At Level 25, choose between +1.5% Max Health Sun Ray Damage (increasing max health damage from 6% to 7.5% per second) or +1000 Icarus Dive Cast Range (increasing range from 1400 to 2400). Pick the Sun Ray damage against high-HP strength heroes, turning it into a tank-melting ability. Pick the dive range for unparalleled initiation, escape, and repositioning, allowing you to dive over entire teamfights.
search_text: Phoenix talent level 25 left +1.5% Max Health Sun Ray Damage 7.5% per second melt tanks right +1000 Icarus Dive Cast Range 2400 range initiation escape reposition
